help.title: Help
help.basic1: Upload one or more video files (.mp4 / .mkv / .avi) and subtitle files (.srt). Each video can have its own subtitle assigned — videos without subtitles auto-trigger Whisper transcription. Use the "Output Mode" toggle to merge all into one deck or generate independent decks per video. Optionally configure AI (API key, language pair) in the right panel.
help.basic2: Apply rule filters (duration, exclude words) or click "AI Screen" to intelligently select sentences worth learning.
help.basic3: Choose purpose (Grammar / Vocabulary), click to annotate — AI generates translations, notes, and vocabulary definitions.
help.basic4: Select card structure (Sentence / Vocab) and visual theme (built-in or custom), fine-tune CSS variables like colors/fonts/spacing, preview cards, then download .apkg or sync directly to Anki via AnkiConnect.
help.advancedBatch1: Multi-video is always available — after uploading the first video, an "Add More" drop zone stays visible for easy batch import
help.advancedBatch2: Assign subtitles per video; videos without subtitles auto-trigger Whisper transcription. Remove individual videos at any time.
help.advancedBatch3: Merge mode (default): all videos' cards combined into one deck. Independent mode: each video gets its own deck. The progress bar shows "Video X/N" in real time.
help.advancedAi1: Two-phase AI workflow: Screening (learning value assessment) → Annotation (translation + notes)
help.advancedAi2: Both phase prompts independently customizable with preset templates
help.advancedAi3: Grammar & Patterns mode: focuses on grammar structures, sentence patterns, practical expressions
help.advancedAi4: Vocabulary mode: focuses on word extraction, POS definitions, contextual memory. Supports OpenAI / DeepSeek / Ollama compatible APIs; config auto-saved in browser.
help.advancedAi5: Annotation is optional: skip to generate basic cards (with text, audio, screenshots) without AI annotations.
help.advancedTheme1: 4 built-in visual themes: Classic, Minimal Immersive, Netflix Stills, Dictionary — each with unique CSS + HTML
help.advancedTheme2: 2 card structures: Sentence Card (screenshot+audio → text+translation+notes) and Vocab Card (word → definition+example)
help.advancedTheme3: CSS Variable Editor: fine-tune built-in theme colors, fonts, sizes, spacing, and shadows with live preview; changes saved per theme and auto-injected into .apkg and AnkiConnect sync
help.advancedTheme4: Custom Theme Import: upload a ZIP package (theme.json + front.html + back.html + style.css) for fully custom HTML/CSS card templates; template variables use lowercase ({{sentence}} etc.) and auto-map to Anki field names
help.advancedTheme5: Live preview with flip animation and audio playback before generation
help.advancedTheme6: AI Style Generator: describe your desired card style in natural language (e.g. "Japanese magazine style, cream background"), and AI generates matching CSS with iterative refinement
help.advancedExport1: AnkiConnect Sync: send cards directly to Anki without manual .apkg import; auto-skips duplicates
help.advancedExport2: .apkg Download: standard Anki deck file for manual import
help.advancedExport3: CSV Export (utf-8-sig, Excel-ready) / JSON Export (complete data) / ZIP Export (bundles CSV + audio + screenshots)
help.advancedExport4: Basic processing works without AI (cards include original text, audio, screenshots; translations/notes left empty)
help.advancedSubtitle1: Upload .srt subtitle files (per-video assignment supported), or auto-extract embedded soft subtitles from video
help.advancedSubtitle2: Whisper transcription (faster-whisper): supports Chinese/English/Japanese/Korean and more, 5 model sizes from tiny to large
help.advancedSubtitle3: Model auto-downloads on first use via China-optimized mirror (hf-mirror.com), with automatic fallback. Offline import supported: place model.bin and other files in %APPDATA%/ClipLingo/models/{model_name}/. Larger = more accurate but slower. Transcription is cancellable with timeout protection.
help.advancedCard1: Audio clips with configurable padding (default ±200ms); audio track extracted first for efficient slicing
help.advancedCard2: Card front fallback: when Screenshot/Audio/Word field is empty, the original sentence text is shown instead
help.advancedCard3: Bidirectional learned words sync: extract learned words from Anki, auto-skip during screening; local SQLite tracking also supported
help.advancedShortcut1: Press Esc to exit the application

step1.title: Prepare Materials
step1.ffmpegHelp: Video processing requires ffmpeg. Please install ffmpeg and add it to your system PATH.
step1.batchDropPrompt: Click or drag to add video & subtitle files
step1.batchDropHint: Supports selecting multiple files, auto-match subtitles by filename
step1.whisperModelHint: Select Whisper model (auto-download on first use)
step1.modelListFailed: Failed to fetch model list, does not affect usage
step1.subtitlesReadyGoNext: Subtitles ready, proceed to next step

step2.title: AI Screening
step2.excludeWordsPlaceholder: Comma separated, e.g.: oh, well, yeah
step2.correctText: Allow AI to correct subtitle text
step2.screeningDone: Screening complete — {{count}} recommended. Confirm selection, then proceed to annotation.
step2.preheatDone: Preprocessing complete, annotation will display instantly

step3.title: AI Annotation
step3.modeGrammarDesc: Focus on grammar structures, sentence patterns, practical expressions
step3.modeVocabDesc: Focus on word extraction, POS definitions, contextual memory
step3.annotatingHint: After annotation, you can preview cards and generate the deck

step4.title: Style Selection
step4.noAiNote: No AI annotation used — will generate basic cards (with original text, audio, screenshots; no translations/notes)

whisper.base: Fast, good enough for daily use

prompt.grammarScreenBody:
You are a {source_language} textbook editor. Evaluate each subtitle line for learning value:

Criteria:
- Has clear grammar points (tenses, clauses, subjunctive mood, etc.)
- Has practical expressions or fixed collocations
- Content is meaningful (not simple greetings like 'okay', 'yeah', 'uh-huh')
- Has cultural context or situational meaning
prompt.vocabScreenBody:
You are a {source_language} vocabulary teaching expert. Judge whether each subtitle is worth learning as vocabulary material:

Criteria:
- Contains high-frequency core vocabulary or academic words
- Contains valuable phrasal verbs, prepositional collocations, or idioms
- Contains examples of polysemy or uncommon word usage
- Words are easier to understand and memorize in context
- Content is meaningful (not simple greetings like 'okay', 'yeah', 'uh-huh')

For include=true items, the notes field should note: key word - part of speech - definition; mark phrases as a whole unit
prompt.grammarAnnotateBody: You are a {source_language} textbook editor. Provide translations and grammar notes for the subtitle list (pre-screened for learning value).
prompt.vocabAnnotateBody: You are a {target_language} vocabulary teaching expert. Provide translations and vocabulary notes for the subtitle list (pre-screened for learning value).
